data.content:
participant contribution,positive feedback,reference material,testing example	Meeting Transcript
Speaker 1
Speaker 1 offers a transcript from an internal project as a practical example of good protocol generation for the team to study.<SEP>Speaker 1 contributed to the meeting transcript by expressing satisfaction with the outcome and bidding farewell.

tool usage,transcription service	Speech To Text Ru
Transcribed Meeting
The content of the meeting transcript was generated using the Speech To Text Ru service.

closing remarks,participant interaction	Meeting Transcript
Speaker 2
Speaker 2 participated in the meeting transcript by exchanging farewells with Speaker 1.

administrative action,workflow transition	Meeting Transcript
Speaker 4
Speaker 4 contributed to the transcript by noting a punctuation update and transitioning the group back to work.

employment,representation	Forus Group Companies
Nikita Kasyanov
Nikita Kasyanov acts as the representative for the Forus Group Companies during the meeting.

leadership,team membership	Dream Team
Nikita Gritsanyuk
Nikita Gritsanyuk serves as the captain of the Dream Team.

representation,team membership	Ekaterina
KFK Team
Ekaterina attends the meeting on behalf of the KFK Team.

leadership,team membership	Optima Team
Yakov Vaygus
Yakov Vaygus serves as the captain of the Optima Team.

execution,responsibility	Junior System Analyst
Meeting Protocol Preparation Process
The Junior System Analyst is responsible for executing the protocol preparation process by listening to recordings and drafting documents.

quality assurance,review	Meeting Protocol Preparation Process
Senior System Analyst
The Senior System Analyst reviews the work of the junior analyst to ensure no details were missed or incorrectly recorded in the protocol.

input data,source material	Meeting Protocol Preparation Process
Video Conference Recordings
Video conference recordings serve as the essential input for the protocol preparation process conducted by analysts.

client relation,project assignment	Dream Team
Nikita Kasyanov
Nikita Kasyanov discusses the task with the Dream Team captain regarding their assigned work for Forus Group Companies.

hierarchy,quality assurance,quality control,review relationship,sequential workflow,supervision	Junior System Analyst
Senior System Analyst
The Junior System Analyst produces initial drafts which are subsequently reviewed by the Senior System Analyst to catch missed details.<SEP>The Senior System Analyst reviews the work of the Junior System Analyst to catch missed details and correct inaccuracies in the protocol draft.<SEP>The Junior System Analyst passes the refined draft to the Senior System Analyst for terminology verification and comprehensive proofreading.

client concern,project context	Meeting Protocol Preparation Process
Nikita Kasyanov
Nikita Kasyanov is the client stakeholder for whom the protocol preparation challenges and process are being discussed as a problem statement to be solved.

inquiry,participation status	Speaker 4
Tigran
Speaker 4 inquires whether Tigran plans to connect to the meeting.

deployment location,organizational affiliation	Спикер 1
Форус
Спикер 1 indicates that the solution will be deployed within the internal network of Форус.

presentation,terminology display	Conceptual Dictionary
Никита
Никита presented conceptual dictionaries containing specific terms to the group.

availability constraint,scheduling impact	Client Users
Meeting Schedule
The availability of end users (clients) directly influences the scheduling gaps and delays between meetings in the project plan.

constraint adherence,domain specificity	1С Sphere
LM-ка
The LM-ка must strictly adhere to the specific concepts and terminology defined within the 1С sphere as per the client's requirements.

transcription inquiry,workflow dependency	Video Meeting Recording
Спикер 7
Спикер 7 inquires whether the team must manually transcribe video meeting recordings or if it will be provided automatically.

automated processing,data input	AI Agent
Protocol
The Protocol serves as the input for the AI agent, which transforms it into an analytical note after verification.

personal use,tool utilization	Chat GPT
Спикер 1
Employees at Форус utilize Chat GPT for non-project tasks where personal data is not a concern.

implementation requirement,system monitoring	Logging Mechanism
Спикер 3
Спикер 3 questions whether logging mechanisms need to be configured to ensure proper product usage tracking.

information retrieval,task responsibility	Junior Analyst
Video Meeting Recording
The Junior Analyst relies on the meeting recording to recall discussion details and prepare the protocol.

development,implementation	IT Support Assistant
Laboratory
The Laboratory developed and implemented the internal IT support assistant for employee use.

affiliation,education	IVE
Спикер 3
Спикер 3 is a student at IVE and seeks guidance on technical implementation details like logging.

automated transcription,platform capability	Video Meeting Recording
ZOOM
Platforms like ZOOM offer agents that can automatically generate transcripts from video recordings, contrasting with the organization's current limitations.

security constraint,tool usage	Telemost
Спикер 1
The organization primarily operates within Telemost for meetings, which prevents the use of external cloud-based transcription tools due to data privacy concerns.

design specification,functional requirement	Chatbot
Speaker 1
Speaker 1 defines the intended behavior and scope of the Chatbot as a non-editing navigator for documents.

context building,data source	Chatbot
Project Documentation
The Chatbot utilizes Project Documentation to accumulate context and answer queries about past discussions.

compliance constraint,data security	NDA Agreement
Speaker 1
Speaker 1 states that the NDA agreement prevents external data transfer without encryption.

operational logic,scope definition	Chatbot
Context Accumulation
The Chatbot's functionality relies on Context Accumulation, which can be scoped to a single project or extended to related projects.

model selection criteria,regulatory inquiry	Federal Laws (FZ)
Speaker 2
Speaker 2 discusses how Federal Laws might restrict the choice of language models to specific domestic options like GigaChat.

internal tool context,legislative constraint	GigaChat
Speaker 4
Speaker 4 questions if legislative constraints are necessary for an internal tool, contrasting with the need for models like GigaChat.

future development,integration capability	Information Technology Department
Speaker 1
Speaker 1 notes that the Information Technology Department can handle API integration into internal systems later in the process.

performance metric,validation criteria	Accuracy Requirement
Speaker 1
Speaker 1 defines the Accuracy Requirement as a measure of how well the model reflects meeting details and avoids misinterpretations.

flexibility,integration method	APIs
Chatbot
The Chatbot currently utilizes APIs to connect with various models, allowing for future replacement with internal APIs.

error checking,validation criteria	Chatbot
Missed Details
The system is designed to identify and quantify missed details in meeting discussions as part of its accuracy validation process.

data transfer constraint,security protocol	Encrypted Data
Speaker 1
Speaker 1 specifies that external data transfer is permitted only if the data is encrypted to satisfy security constraints.

terminology check,validation criteria	Chatbot
Incorrect Terms
The system checks for misinterpreted concepts or incorrectly selected terms within the document context.

data preparation,privacy protection	Sanitized Documentation
Speaker 1
Speaker 1 provided anonymized documentation examples to ensure no personal or organizational data leaks during the task demonstration.

future configuration,system integration	Chatbot
Internal APIs
The Chatbot is designed to replace current API connections with internal company APIs upon final system implementation.

dual responsibility,role definition	Functional Customer
Speaker 1
Speaker 1 acts as both a functional customer and an IT specialist within the project context.

error metric,quality assessment	Chatbot
Red Pages
The system evaluates performance by counting "red pages" (pages with significant errors) as a metric of incorrect interpretations.

clarification exchange,collaboration,collaborative discussion,meeting participants	Nikita
Roman
Nikita and Roman engage in a dialogue to clarify definitions, with Roman subsequently contributing key information that prompts a response from the main speaker.<SEP>Nikita and Roman are colleagues present in the discussion who are asked to wait for their turn to speak.

development verification,inquiry	Ekaterina
Video Recording
Ekaterina inquires about the existence of a video recording to assist with transcription verification.

implementation challenge,implementation gap,resource gap,resource limitation	1C RP
IT Department
The IT Department may lack the resources to implement large products like 1C RP independently, necessitating external help or functional customers.<SEP>The IT department often lacks the internal resources to implement large products like 1C RP independently.

affiliation,leadership	Center For Automation And Support
Speaker 1
Speaker 1 represents the Center For Automation And Support and discusses its operational capacity.

domain knowledge,requirements gathering	Business Process
Functional Customer
Functional customers provide critical details about business processes (e.g., coal burning efficiency) that IT staff may not know.

infrastructure,technical requirement	Containerization
Scalability
Horizontal scalability is discussed as a potential technical solution involving containerization support.

error analysis,metric evaluation	Accuracy Calculation
Document Review Process
The accuracy of the document review is calculated by comparing incorrect pages to total pages (e.g., 1 red page out of 4 equals 75% accuracy).

dual capacity,role overlap	Functional Customer
System Analyst
Junior system analysts are explicitly identified as potential functional customers within the organization.

domain operation,end user	1C RP
Accountant
Accountants are primary users of 1C RP for accounting tasks, distinct from IT staff who implement it.

access rights,management use	1C RP
Director
Directors may access the system to view reports or manage operations, serving as functional customers.

domain knowledge,inventory tracking	Bakery Owner
Pirog
The bakery owner tracks details of pirogs (costs, sourcing) which defines their role as a functional customer for inventory systems.

assumption recording,inaccuracy risk	Meeting Protocol
Project Team Members
Project team members risk adding unverified assumptions to the Meeting Protocol if they do not confirm discussions with the customer.

responsibility assignment,verification	Customer
Meeting Protocol
The Customer must verify the Meeting Protocol to ensure responsibilities and answers are correctly recorded as agreed upon.

documentation flow,input source	Analytical Note
Junior System Analyst
The Junior System Analyst contributes information from meeting protocols that is consolidated into the Analytical Note.

automation,draft generation	Meeting Protocol
Meeting Recording Assistant
The Meeting Recording Assistant is intended to generate a preliminary version of the Meeting Protocol based on audio or text recordings.

finalization,oversight	Analytical Note
Senior System Analyst
The Senior System Analyst oversees the creation of the Analytical Note, ensuring it accurately reflects the survey findings derived from protocols.

aggregation,component	Analytical Note
Meeting Protocol
The Meeting Protocols serve as the primary source documents that are combined to form the final Analytical Note.

comparison,page count distinction	Analytical Note
Survey Report
The Analytical Note is distinguished from the Survey Report by its length, with the former being under 50 pages and the latter exceeding 50 pages.

clarification inquiry,document distinction	Analytical Note
Nikita
Nikita questions whether the Analytical Note and Survey Report are the same entity or different deliverables.

clarification,contribution	Roman
Survey Report
Roman provides a crucial clarification regarding the definition of the Survey Report in relation to the Analytical Note.

document definition,generation requirement,structure,user agreement	Analytical Note
Speaker 1
Speaker 1 defines the Analytical Note as a document typically under 50 pages that structures information from protocols.<SEP>Speaker 1 emphasizes that a protocol must be agreed upon with the client before an Analytical Note can be generated by the system.

automation,organizational context	Forest
Internal Projects
The company Forest conducts internal projects focused on automation where analytical notes are generated as part of the process.

document comparison,volume difference	Speaker 1
Survey Report
Speaker 1 contrasts the Survey Report with the Analytical Note, noting it is significantly larger (50-200+ pages).

source linkage,traceability	Functional Requirements Registry
Protocol
The Functional Requirements Registry must include links to specific protocols where requirements were discussed for verification.

content composition,formatting	Analytical Note
Business Process Description
The Business Process Description is a key component of the Analytical Note, often formatted as tables or requiring PPM schemes.

capability limitation,task delegation	Assistant
PPM Scheme
The Assistant cannot draw PPM schemes; this task requires a System Analyst to ensure accuracy and complexity handling.

client validation,review process	Functional Requirements Registry
System Analyst
The content of the registry is reviewed with clients to confirm requirements were discussed, ensuring project alignment.

data extraction,information source	Analytical Note
MTR File
Data from the MTR file regarding current views and naming conventions can be included in the Analytical Note.

content integration,supplementary information	Analytical Note
Project Charter
Additional details from the Project Charter may be integrated into the Analytical Note when available.

discussion contribution	Roman
Speaker 1
Roman contributed a key detail that helped clarify the distinction between document types in the discussion.

data flow,integration direction	1C ERP
New System
Data flows from the new system into 1C ERP specifically for updating catalog information.

classification,data grouping	Project Context
Russian Classifier
The Russian Classifier constitutes a distinct category of catalogs that were highlighted in the project requirements.

coexistence,data migration	Legacy System
New System
Data continues to be sent from the new system to the legacy system during the transition period.

future state definition,validation	Customer
Target Model
The Customer validates the Target Model to confirm it aligns with their vision of the updated system.

content structure,specificity	Analytical Note
Attribute Composition
The Attribute Composition is included within the Analytical Note but may not be present in all projects depending on requirements.

review process,workflow handoff	AI Assistant
Junior System Analyst
The AI Assistant generates a draft protocol that serves as the starting point for the Junior System Analyst to refine and verify content.

client compliance,hierarchical oversight	Project Team Leader
Senior System Analyst
The Senior System Analyst ensures technical accuracy while the Project Team Leader monitors adherence to client-specific formatting and structural requirements.

data processing,input source	AI Assistant
Meeting Recording
The AI Assistant utilizes the meeting recording as its primary input to generate the draft protocol.

error correction,timestamp review	Draft Protocol
Junior System Analyst
The Junior System Analyst addresses specific inaccuracies and reviews uncertain sections marked by timestamps within the draft protocol.

document generation,prerequisite dependency	Analytical Note
Finalized Protocol
The Finalized Protocol is a mandatory prerequisite that must be approved before the team can begin preparing the Analytical Note.

collaboration tool,communication channel	Bitrix
Junior System Analyst
The Junior System Analyst utilizes Bitrix for private messaging or chat-based interactions to exchange documents and questions with team members.

format adherence,stakeholder management	Client Requirements
Project Team Leader
The Project Team Leader ensures the protocol aligns with client requirements and maintains the correct document structure based on client communication.

coordination platform,group management	Project Team Leader
Telegram
The Project Team Leader suggests creating a Telegram group to facilitate direct communication and query resolution among all project teams.

data comparison,reliability assessment	Meeting Recording
Transcription
The text discusses a comparison between meeting recordings and transcriptions, noting that recordings are more reliably available than transcriptions for analysis purposes.

knowledge gap,scope limitation	Architecture Details
Project Team Leader
The Project Team Leader is noted to lack deep understanding of technical architecture details, focusing instead on client requirements and general structure.

target audience,user experience	Junior Analyst
Speaker 4
The system should be configured to allow Junior Analysts to quickly perceive contradictions and nuances in the document output.

target audience,user experience	Senior Analyst
Speaker 4
The system must enable Senior Analysts to rapidly understand document content through effective configuration.

escalation path,resolution	Client
Junior Analyst
Analysts can contact the Client via chat if they identify open questions or contradictions within the documents.

design proposal,functionality	Chatbot Interface
Speaker 1
Speaker 1 proposes a chatbot interface similar to ChatGPT or DeepS for interacting with documents and querying past discussions.

feedback loop,machine learning	Analytical Note
Model
Refined analytical notes can be fed back into the model to train it on new data and improve performance.

design preference,editing capability	Single Canvas Interface
Speaker 2
Speaker 2 advocates for a single canvas approach that allows direct document editing, similar to Canva's functionality.

content inclusion,document structure	Meeting Protocol
Open Questions
The Meeting Protocol may include a section for Open Questions, though this section is not always present if all issues are resolved.

functionality,output generation	Analytical Note
Chatbot Interface
The chat interface allows users to request the generation of analytical notes based on meeting protocols and context.

design inspiration,layout comparison	LM Notebook
Speaker 1
Speaker 1 compares the proposed system's potential layout (left docs, center chat, right outputs) to the design used in LM Notebook.

document structure,mandatory requirement	Date Field
Meeting Protocol
The date field is an essential component required for every meeting protocol document.

document structure,mandatory requirement	Agenda
Meeting Protocol
An agenda must be included in the protocol to define the scope of the meeting.

document structure,mandatory requirement	Meeting Protocol
Participants
Listing participants is a compulsory element for validating and documenting the meeting attendees.

optional content,terminology	Definitions Section
Meeting Protocol
The definitions section is an optional addition to the protocol used to clarify specific terms when needed.

standard practice,validation	Meeting Protocol
Stamp Of Agreement
A stamp of agreement is typically required on protocols to signify formal approval and consensus.

conditional logic,content creation	Definitions Section
Junior Analyst
Junior analysts have the discretion to omit the definitions section if they determine it is not necessary for a specific protocol.

development support,information request	Speaker 7
Video Recording
Speaker 7 inquires about the existence of a meeting recording to use as a reference for developing and verifying system features like transcription.

data refinement,feedback mechanism	Analytical Note
Model Training
The analytical note serves as training data that can be fed back into the model to improve its future performance and accuracy.

confidentiality,resource sourcing	Internal Project
Speaker 1
Speaker 1 indicates that video recordings can be obtained from internal projects because external client data cannot be shared.

security concern,testing access	Speaker 3
Telegram Group
Speaker 3 questions the security implications of making public links available for testing on the Telegram group or similar platforms.

mandatory field definition	Project Protocol
Speaker 2
Speaker 2 clarifies that Project Protocols are mandatory inputs required to generate an Analytical Note or Report on Inspection.

collaborative learning,follow-up planning	Optima Team
Speaker 4
Speaker 4 organizes the Optima Team for transcription practice via a shared group and proposes a follow-up meeting to resolve questions by Saturday.

data dependency,system requirement	Speaker 1
Transcription
Speaker 1 asserts that working with recordings to generate protocols is an essential task, whereas text-only input is not sufficient for the core function.

role definition,testing context	Meeting Session
Roman
Roman identifies that the current meeting session functions as a practical test where participants act as analysts interacting with a client representative.

mandatory field definition	Report On Inspection
Speaker 2
Speaker 2 confirms that protocols are mandatory requirements for generating both Analytical Notes and Reports on Inspection.

documentation,output	Analytical Note
Survey Stage
The Survey Stage results in the creation of an Analytical Note which documents findings about the customer's situation.

dependency,risk propagation	Development Stage
Survey Stage
The quality of the Development Stage is directly dependent on the Survey Stage; poor surveys lead to incorrect development and financial losses.

input,preparation	Modeling Stage
Technical Specification
The Modeling Stage utilizes survey data to prepare the Technical Specification (TZ) for developers.

automation,efficiency	AI Assistant
Survey Stage
The AI Assistant is proposed to automate routine tasks within the Survey Stage to reduce time consumption.

guidance,suggestion	AI Assistant
BPMN Schema
The AI Assistant can recommend that an analyst create a BPMN schema based on text descriptions of processes.

context,terminology	1C Domain
Forum (Customer)
The Forum operates within the 1C Domain, requiring strict adherence to its specific conceptual vocabulary in project documents.

consequence,risk mitigation	Legal Dispute
Survey Stage
Inadequate execution of the Survey Stage increases the likelihood of legal disputes between the executor and the customer over ambiguous requirements.

synonymy,terminology	Research Phase
Survey Stage
The Survey Stage is also referred to as the Research Phase; they are synonyms used interchangeably by project members to describe the investigation of customer needs and problems (Ziz).

design,phase decomposition	Modeling Stage
Preparation Of TZ
The Modeling Stage is divided into two distinct sub-sectors: the Preparation of Technical Specifications (TZ) and the Design phase.

internal collaboration,team composition	Executor
Project Group
The Project Group consists solely of representatives from the Executor side.

phase decomposition,system creation	Design Phase
Modeling Stage
The Modeling Stage includes a specific section called the Design Phase, which focuses on creating the future system's architecture for developers.

collaboration,external communication	Client
External Team
The External Team includes both the Client and the Executors for broader project interaction.

data input,processing	Bot Assistant
Transcripts
The Bot Assistant processes transcribed data from client interviews to generate protocols.

internal channel,team identification	Dream Team
Telegram Group
The Dream Team (or Optima) utilizes a dedicated Telegram group for their specific team activities.

contextual reference,terminology extraction	Bot Assistant
Project Charter
The Bot Assistant uses the Project Charter to extract specific terminology and details for protocol generation.

document creation,output generation	Bot Assistant
Meeting Protocols
The Bot Assistant generates Meeting Protocols based on interview context and project documentation.

client requirements,conditional structure	Forum Project
Functional Template
The Functional Template can be designed as a "floating" template for the Forum Project to accommodate varying document section requirements.

error reduction,goal alignment	Bot Assistant
PDF Document
The Bot Assistant is intended to fulfill the goal in the PDF document of eliminating human errors during report creation.

decision support,information retrieval	Analyst
Regulations
Analysts consult Regulations to find answers and resolve questions during meetings or documentation work.

context supply,data provision	Client
Product Specifications
The Client provides product specifications (e.g., from 1C) as additional context for the project.

human error analysis,system limitation	Bot Assistant
Speaker 1
Speaker 1 argues that while humans make errors in reports, the bot must be designed to detect contradictions arising from changing decisions within a meeting.

conflict detection,consistency check	Contradictions
Meeting Protocols
The system must be capable of identifying contradictions within Meeting Protocols where decisions may have changed or conflict with previous statements.

conditional logic,template design	Functional Template
Speaker 2
Speaker 2 suggests using a functional template with floating sections to accommodate varying project requirements like those in the Forum Project.

format specification,structural definition	Output Document
Speaker 4
Speaker 4 defines that the output document requires a fixed structural template (functional sections) but flexible internal content.
